title: Introducing issue2pr
summary: Ever had an issue on GitHub, and then you fixed the bug? Opened a brand new pull request, which becomes a new issue, and then you have to remember to link the two via a comment, or manually go back and close the issue as well? Turns out that GitHub has the power to turn Issues…
content:
Ever had an issue on GitHub, and then you fixed the bug? Opened a brand new pull request, which becomes a new issue, and then you have to remember to link the two via a comment, or manually go back and close the issue as well?

Turns out that GitHub has the power to turn Issues into Pull Requests in the API, but not in the web UI. So I wrote a tiny web app that lets you use the API call yourself: http://issue2pr.herokuapp.com/

Of course, the code is up on GitHub, here: https://github.com/steveklabnik/issue2pr

Issues (and pull requests) welcome!
